stories.text: As an AI agent, I want my provider requests to use a persistent WebSocket connection from a pool so that I don't pay 1-2 seconds of TLS handshake overhead on every request.
As an AI agent, I want the WebSocket connection to reconnect seamlessly when the 60-minute server limit is reached so that long sessions complete without transport errors.
As an AI agent, I want provider requests to fall back to HTTP automatically if WebSocket is unavailable so that no request is lost due to transport issues.
As an AI agent running concurrently with siblings, I want my own WebSocket connection from the pool so that I don't block or serialize against other agents.
As an AI agent, I want the provider to recover WebSocket connectivity after transient failures so that I'm not stuck on HTTP for the rest of the session.
As an AI agent, I want the WebSocket transport to automatically prime the server's prompt cache on fresh connections so that time-to-first-token is reduced without any action on my part.
As an AI agent, I want the provider to acknowledge completed responses on the WebSocket so that the server can manage connection state correctly.
As the workflow engine, I want the provider to handle transport selection automatically so that workflow steps don't need transport-level configuration.
As the workflow engine, I want long-running workflow sessions to maintain provider connectivity across many agent turns so that multi-hour workflows complete reliably.
As a developer, I want HTTP and WebSocket transports to share the same event mapping code so that provider behavior is consistent and changes are made in one place.
As a developer, I want the HTTP transport extracted to its own file so that HTTP and WS transports are structurally symmetric self-contained modules.
As a developer, I want WS transport debug output in the same JSONL format as HTTP so that debug-api dumps are transport-agnostic.
As an operator, I want transport fallback events logged with reasons so that I can diagnose why the provider switched from WebSocket to HTTP.
As an operator, I want connection lifecycle events logged at debug level so that I can trace WebSocket establishment, reconnection, and closure.
